section: Control and Coordination: Neural and Hormonal Systems

concepts.lesson: The neuron is the structural unit of the nervous system. Information travels as an electrical impulse from the dendrites, through the cell body, and along the axon. At the axonal end, the impulse triggers the release of neurotransmitters that cross the gap (synapse) to reach the next neuron.
concepts.real_example: The lightning-fast transmission of a signal from your toe to your brain when you stub it.
key_points: Synapse ensures one-way flow of information

concepts.lesson: A reflex action is a sudden, involuntary response to a stimulus. To save time, the signal is processed in the spinal cord rather than the brain. The path followed (Receptor $\rightarrow$ Sensory Neuron $\rightarrow$ Spinal Cord $\rightarrow$ Motor Neuron $\rightarrow$ Effector) is the reflex arc.
concepts.real_example: Automatically pulling your hand away after touching a boiling hot pan.
key_points: Brain is informed later for memory

concepts.lesson: The brain is divided into Forebrain (thinking, memory, voluntary actions), Midbrain (visual/auditory reflexes), and Hindbrain. The hindbrain includes the Cerebellum (balance/posture), Medulla (involuntary actions like BP), and Pons.
concepts.real_example: Using your Cerebellum to maintain balance while riding a bicycle.
key_points: Forebrain is the main thinking part
Brain is protected by CSF and the Cranium

concepts.lesson: Plants coordinate movements through growth in response to stimuli. These are called tropisms. Common types include Phototropism (light), Geotropism (gravity), Chemotropism (chemicals), and Hydrotropism (water).
concepts.real_example: A sunflower turning its head to track the sun across the sky.
key_points: Pollen tubes show chemotropism toward the ovule

concepts.lesson: Chemicals produced by plants to coordinate growth and development. Auxins promote cell elongation, Gibberellins help in stem growth, Cytokinins promote cell division, and Abscisic Acid acts as a growth inhibitor.
concepts.real_example: Leaves falling off in autumn due to the action of Abscisic acid.
key_points: Auxins cause shoots to bend toward light
Cytokinins are high in fruits and seeds
Abscisic acid causes wilting of leaves

concepts.lesson: Endocrine glands secrete hormones directly into the blood. The Pituitary gland (Master Gland) controls others. Thyroid produces Thyroxine (metabolism), Pancreas produces Insulin (blood sugar), and Adrenal glands produce Adrenaline (stress response).
concepts.real_example: Adrenaline increasing your heart rate before a big stage performance.
